title: SQLite Task Manager
description: Build a simple task manager using SQLite.
problemStatement:
Create a script that manages tasks using SQLite. The database should have a `tasks` table with columns: `id` (integer primary key autoincrement), `title` (text not null), `completed` (integer default 0, where 0=false, 1=true).

Implement functions (using async/await style via `util.promisify` or callbacks) to:
- `addTask(title)` – inserts a new task.
- `listTasks()` – shows all tasks.
- `completeTask(id)` – sets completed=1 for a task.
- `deleteTask(id)` – removes a task.

Write a small CLI that accepts commands (e.g., `node task.js add "Buy milk"`, `node task.js list`, `node task.js complete 1`, `node task.js delete 1`). Use `process.argv` to parse commands.
hints: Create table if not exists using `db.run`.
For async/await, you can promisify `db.run`, `db.all`, `db.get` using `util.promisify` or wrap in promises.
